definition: Non-GAAP earnings adjustments expanded 40-60% during the tightening cycle, with the S&P 500 GAAP-to-non-GAAP gap widening from ~$18-22/share (2020-21) to ~$28-38/share (2024-25E). Restructuring charges doubled, refinancing costs tripled, goodwill impairments nearly tripled — each directly traceable to rate increases, making ~20-25% of monetary transmission invisible to dominant valuation frameworks.
evidence: Data directly observable in company filings and verifiable from FactSet/S&P Capital IQ
Confirmed as most empirically grounded insight in pair_1 debate
open_questions: How will the gap resolve — compression of non-GAAP or recognition in GAAP?
Does the gap carry information about future earnings revisions?
